facts.text: The 1990s blended indie breakthroughs, big effects-driven blockbusters, and prestige dramas into one of the most varied movie decades.
Goodfellas (1990) became one of the most acclaimed crime films ever made and influenced decades of editing and narration style.
The Silence of the Lambs (1991) won the 'Big Five' Oscars and remains one of the rare thrillers to win Best Picture.
Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991) raised the bar for CGI and action spectacle.
Jurassic Park (1993) changed visual effects forever by combining animatronics and photorealistic computer-generated dinosaurs.
Schindler's List (1993) won Best Picture and Best Director for Steven Spielberg.
Pulp Fiction (1994) helped redefine independent cinema's commercial potential with nonlinear storytelling and quotable dialogue.
The Lion King (1994) became one of Disney's biggest animated hits and a cornerstone of the Disney Renaissance.
Toy Story (1995) was the first fully computer-animated feature film and launched Pixar as a major force in cinema.
Titanic opened in 1997 and tied the all-time Oscar win record with 11 Academy Awards.
The Matrix premiered in 1999 and changed action filmmaking with bullet time, cyberpunk style, and philosophical sci-fi.
Saving Private Ryan (1998) became famous for its brutally realistic D-Day opening sequence.
Fight Club (1999) had a mixed initial response but later grew into one of the most discussed cult films of its era.
The Blair Witch Project (1999) showed how a tiny budget and clever marketing could create a massive cultural phenomenon.
The 1990s were also huge for rom-coms, with films like Pretty Woman, Sleepless in Seattle, and Notting Hill becoming favorites.
Jim Carrey became one of the decade's breakout comedy stars through movies like Ace Ventura, The Mask, and Dumb and Dumber.
The decade saw a surge in teen movies including Clueless, 10 Things I Hate About You, and American Pie.
Indie directors like Quentin Tarantino, the Coens, Richard Linklater, and Kevin Smith helped define a new alternative film voice.
CGI advanced rapidly in the 1990s, but many of the decade's best effects still relied on strong practical foundations.
For many fans, the 1990s were the perfect balance between old-school filmmaking craft and modern digital possibility.
Goodfellas (1990) became one of the most acclaimed crime films ever made.
